source: Invest Like the Best — Tech and consumer growth investing (EP.149)
summary: Global video game population will eventually encompass all humans on Earth, driven by mobile and Fortnite as new entry points.
summary_extended: Baker argues that the global video game population is undergoing a long-term structural expansion that will eventually encompass all of humanity, representing one of the largest TAM expansions in consumer history. He supports this view by noting that the gaming population has grown 10x over the past 10-15 years, driven by successive waves of new entry points: first Nintendo and arcades, then the iPhone democratizing mobile gaming, and most recently Fortnite attracting entirely new demographics. Currently fewer than one-third of Earth's population plays games, leaving a massive untapped addressable market. Baker is 'reasonably confident' that within his lifetime every person on Earth will play video games in some form, making this a decades-long secular growth thesis rather than a near-term trade. The investment implication is broadly bullish on the gaming ecosystem, but Baker does not name specific companies in this segment, making it a thematic macro view rather than a stock-specific call.
detail: The global gaming population has 10X'd in 10-15 years. The industry had one entry point (Nintendo/arcades), then the iPhone created a second, and Fortnite created a third. Currently less than one-third of Earth's population plays games; Baker believes everyone will play in some form within his lifetime.
confidence_evidence: Baker says he is 'reasonably confident' about this statement; framed as a long-term structural view.
quote_fragment: everyone on Planet Earth will play video games in one form or another in my lifetime
